drifts.truth: enableExport defaults to false (PRD R7; ARCHITECTURE flags table; README; CONTEXT)
drifts.detect: code defaults enableExport to true; docs say its default is false

drifts.injected: removed the visibility gating: polling no longer pauses on a hidden tab nor refetches on visibilitychange (now a plain setInterval)
drifts.truth: polling MUST pause while the tab is hidden and refetch when it becomes visible (PRD R4; ARCHITECTURE polling layer; CONTEXT 'pauses on hidden tab'; IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN Phase 4)
drifts.detect: docs say polling pauses on hidden tabs and refetches on visibility; usePolling now just polls on a fixed interval

drifts.truth: OAuth scope = metrics:read (src/auth/oauth.ts SCOPE; README Authentication). The product is read-only (README intro), so a write scope is also semantically wrong.
drifts.detect: ARCHITECTURE requests a metrics:write scope while the code and README use metrics:read only (and the app is read-only)

drifts.injected: claims real-time updates over a WebSocket channel (wss://api.pulse.example.com/live)
drifts.truth: there is no WebSocket anywhere — the app is polling-only (src/hooks/usePolling.ts; ARCHITECTURE polling layer); no ws/wss client, no live channel in code or other docs
drifts.detect: CONTEXT claims a shipped WebSocket live-update channel that does not exist in the code

drifts.class: arch-vs-plan (plan claims component absent from arch/code)
drifts.injected: adds: 'All UI strings are localized via react-i18next (English + Spanish bundles)'
drifts.truth: there is no i18n/localization — no react-i18next dependency in package.json, no locale bundles, components use hardcoded English strings; ARCHITECTURE never mentions i18n
drifts.detect: the plan claims a react-i18next localization deliverable that the architecture and code never realize

drifts.truth: default date range = '7d' / last 7 days (PRD R5; ARCHITECTURE; src/config.ts defaultDateRange; CONTEXT)
drifts.detect: README says the default date range is 30d while code and the other docs say 7d

drifts.truth: session is stored under sessionStorage key 'pulse.session' (ARCHITECTURE auth flow; README Authentication; IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN Phase 3)
